children.heading: 700 MHz band
children.label: 1 700 MHz band
children.text: The term “700 MHz band” means the portion of the electromagnetic spectrum between the frequencies from 698 megahertz to 806 megahertz.

children.heading: 700 MHz D block spectrum
children.label: 2 700 MHz D block spectrum
children.text: The term “700 MHz D block spectrum” means the portion of the electromagnetic spectrum between the frequencies from 758 megahertz to 763 megahertz and between the frequencies from 788 megahertz to 793 megahertz.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Assistant Secretary
children.label: 4 Assistant Secretary
children.text: The term “Assistant Secretary” means the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information.

children.heading: Board
children.label: 5 Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the Board of the First Responder Network Authority established under .section 1424(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a full-power television station; or

children.label: B
children.text: a low-power television station that has been accorded primary status as a Class A television licensee under section 73.6001(a) of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.heading: Broadcast television spectrum
children.label: 7 Broadcast television spectrum
children.text: The term “broadcast television spectrum” means the portions of the electromagnetic spectrum between the frequencies from 54 megahertz to 72 megahertz, from 76 megahertz to 88 megahertz, from 174 megahertz to 216 megahertz, and from 470 megahertz to 698 megahertz.

children.label: A
children.text: a data service;

children.label: B
children.text: provided for profit; and

children.label: C
children.text: available to the public or such classes of eligible users as to be effectively available to a substantial portion of the public, as specified by regulation by the Commission.

children.heading: Commercial mobile service
children.label: 9 Commercial mobile service
children.text: The term “commercial mobile service” has the meaning given such term in .section 332 of this title

children.heading: Commercial standards
children.label: 10 Commercial standards
children.text: The term “commercial standards” means the technical standards followed by the commercial mobile service and commercial mobile data service industries for network, device, and Internet Protocol connectivity. Such term includes standards developed by the Third Generation Partnership Project (3GPP), the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), the Alliance for Telecommunications Industry Solutions (ATIS), the Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF), and the International Telecommunication Union (ITU).

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 11 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Federal Communications Commission.

children.heading: Core network
children.label: 12 Core network
children.text: The term “core network” means the core network described in .section 1422(b)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: through voice, text, or video and related data; and

children.label: B
children.text: nonhuman-initiated automatic event alerts, such as alarms, telematics, or sensor data, which may also include real-time voice, text, or video communications.

children.label: A
children.text: from 763 megahertz to 768 megahertz;

children.label: B
children.text: from 793 megahertz to 798 megahertz;

children.label: C
children.text: from 768 megahertz to 769 megahertz; and

children.label: D
children.text: from 798 megahertz to 799 megahertz.

children.heading: First Responder Network Authority
children.label: 15 First Responder Network Authority
children.text: The term “First Responder Network Authority” means the First Responder Network Authority established under .section 1424 of this title

children.heading: Forward auction
children.label: 16 Forward auction
children.text: The term “forward auction” means the portion of an incentive auction of broadcast television spectrum under .section 1452(c) of this title

children.heading: Incentive auction
children.label: 17 Incentive auction
children.text: The term “incentive auction” means a system of competitive bidding under subparagraph (G) of , as added by section 6402.section 309(j)(8) of this title

children.heading: Interoperability Board
children.label: 18 Interoperability Board
children.text: The term “Interoperability Board” means the Technical Advisory Board for First Responder Interoperability established under .section 1423 of this title

children.heading: Multichannel video programming distributor
children.label: 19 Multichannel video programming distributor
children.text: The term “multichannel video programming distributor” has the meaning given such term in .section 522 of this title

children.heading: Narrowband spectrum
children.label: 20 Narrowband spectrum
children.text: The term “narrowband spectrum” means the portion of the electromagnetic spectrum between the frequencies from 769 megahertz to 775 megahertz and between the frequencies from 799 megahertz to 805 megahertz.

children.heading: Nationwide public safety broadband network
children.label: 21 Nationwide public safety broadband network
children.text: The term “nationwide public safety broadband network” means the nationwide, interoperable public safety broadband network described in .section 1422 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: provides standardized interfaces from emergency call and message services to support emergency communications;

children.label: B
children.text: processes all types of emergency calls, including voice, text, data, and multimedia information;

children.label: C
children.text: acquires and integrates additional emergency call data useful to call routing and handling;

children.label: D
children.text: delivers the emergency calls, messages, and data to the appropriate public safety answering point and other appropriate emergency entities;

children.label: E
children.text: supports data or video communications needs for coordinated incident response and management; and

children.label: F
children.text: provides broadband service to public safety answering points or other first responder entities.

children.heading: NIST
children.label: 23 NIST
children.text: The term “NIST” means the National Institute of Standards and Technology.

children.heading: NTIA
children.label: 24 NTIA
children.text: The term “NTIA” means the National Telecommunications and Information Administration.

children.heading: Public safety answering point
children.label: 25 Public safety answering point
children.text: The term “public safety answering point” has the meaning given such term in .section 222 of this title

children.heading: Public safety entity
children.label: 26 Public safety entity
children.text: The term “public safety entity” means an entity that provides public safety services.

children.label: A
children.text: has the meaning given the term in ; andsection 337(f) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: includes services provided by emergency response providers, as that term is defined in .section 101 of title 6

children.heading: Public Safety Trust Fund
children.label: 28 Public Safety Trust Fund
children.text: The term “Public Safety Trust Fund” means the trust fund established under .section 1457(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Radio access network
children.label: 29 Radio access network
children.text: The term “radio access network” means the radio access network described in .section 1422(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Reverse auction
children.label: 30 Reverse auction
children.text: The term “reverse auction” means the portion of an incentive auction of broadcast television spectrum under , in which a broadcast television licensee may submit bids stating the amount it would accept for voluntarily relinquishing some or all of its broadcast television spectrum usage rights.section 1452(a) of this title

children.heading: State
children.label: 31 State
children.text: The term “State” has the meaning given such term in .section 153 of this title

children.heading: Ultra high frequency
children.label: 32 Ultra high frequency
children.text: The term “ultra high frequency” means, with respect to a television channel, that the channel is located in the portion of the electromagnetic spectrum between the frequencies from 470 megahertz to 698 megahertz.

children.heading: Very high frequency
children.label: 33 Very high frequency
children.text: The term “very high frequency” means, with respect to a television channel, that the channel is located in the portion of the electromagnetic spectrum between the frequencies from 54 megahertz to 72 megahertz, from 76 megahertz to 88 megahertz, or from 174 megahertz to 216 megahertz.
